what: ICD10AnalysisToolbox: Convert Tabular ICD-10 Data into Incidence and Prevalence and Run Follow-Up Analysis
title: ICD10AnalysisToolbox: Convert Tabular ICD-10 Data into Incidence and Prevalence and Run Follow-Up Analysis
text: ICD10AnalysisToolbox written streamline analysis tabular ICD-10 data long format: create_tte_icd() adds history event (yes/), well time event contact loss ID set ICD-10 codes choice. time_to_event_phecode() automatically runs create_tte_icd() Phecodes mapping ICD10 codes phewas_analysis_phecodes() runs Cox proportional hazard models available Phecode outcomes visualises PheWAS plot, like : PheWAS plot

title: ICD10AnalysisToolbox: Convert Tabular ICD-10 Data into Incidence and Prevalence and Run Follow-Up Analysis
text: can install development version ICD10AnalysisToolbox GitHub :

title: ICD10AnalysisToolbox: Convert Tabular ICD-10 Data into Incidence and Prevalence and Run Follow-Up Analysis
text: Add outcome consisting multiple ICD-10 codes existing dataframe, e.g. subtypes Myocardial infarction:
code: cohort_table <- create_tte_icd(cohort_table, icd_table_long, tbl_mortality_contact_loss,                                icd_codes = c("I21.0", "I21.1", "I21.2", "I21.3"), variable_name = "STEMI") cohort_table <- create_tte_icd(cohort_table, icd_table_long, tbl_mortality_contact_loss,                                icd_codes = c("I21.4"), variable_name = "NSTEMI")

title: ICD10AnalysisToolbox: Convert Tabular ICD-10 Data into Incidence and Prevalence and Run Follow-Up Analysis

ICD-10-CM to Clinical Classifications Software Refined (CCSR) mapping — DXCCSR
text: data frame 90,768 rows 6 columns: X.ICD.10.CM.CODE. ICD-10 code ICD.10.CM.CODE.DESCRIPTION ICD-10 description X.Default.CCSR.CATEGORY.IP. Default CCSR ID Default.CCSR.CATEGORY.DESCRIPTION.IP Default CCSR description X.CCSR.CATEGORY.1-5 Assigned category categories, descending order relevance Rationale..Default.Assignment Rationale default CCSR code assignment

what: Convert temporal ICD-10 codes into usable information for Cox regression — create_tte_icd
title: Convert temporal ICD-10 codes into usable information for Cox regression — create_tte_icd
text: Convert temporal ICD-10 codes usable information Cox regression

title: Convert temporal ICD-10 codes into usable information for Cox regression — create_tte_icd
code: create_tte_icd(   dataframe_ID_BL,   icd_table_long,   tbl_mortality_contact,   icd_codes = c("I21"),   variable_name = NULL,   censor_time = 5,   no_FU_as_NA = FALSE )

title: Convert temporal ICD-10 codes into usable information for Cox regression — create_tte_icd
text: dataframe_ID_BL dataframe participant ID column. Dataframe gets returned ICD-10 information added icd_table_long long table ICD-10 information, including ID, ICD-10 code date diagnosed tbl_mortality_contact dataframe containing participant ID, baseline visit date last contact/death date columns. icd_codes One multiple ICD-10 codes interest variable_name Desired column names. Defaults concatenated icd_codes censor_time Time events censored

title: Convert temporal ICD-10 codes into usable information for Cox regression — create_tte_icd
text: Returns dataframe_ID_BL, outcome (event yes/+ time event/censoring) prevalence baseline added.

what: Run many regression models quickly, for many response variables. — fast_glm2
title: Run many regression models quickly, for many response variables. — fast_glm2
text: Performs type cross-sectional regression analysis quickly many response variables.

title: Run many regression models quickly, for many response variables. — fast_glm2
code: fast_glm2(   response = NULL,   response_name = NULL,   Xvar = NULL,   dataset = NULL,   adj_vars = NULL,   family = "gaussian",   exponentiate = FALSE,   p_adjust_meth = "fdr",   robust = FALSE,   sort = TRUE,   decimals = 3 )

title: Run many regression models quickly, for many response variables. — fast_glm2
text: response Character vector variable names use response interest separate regression models. response_name Label display response interest Xvar Main predictor used regression. dataset Dataset variables exist. adj_vars Character vector adjustment covariate names. family Family regression model, e.g. 'gaussian', 'binomial', 'poisson'. exponentiate Whether exponentiate estimate model (e.g. logistic Poisson regression). p_adjust_meth Method provide adjustment P-values. robust TRUE, returns robust standard error estimates. sort TRUE, sorts p-value. decimals Specifies number decimals round estimates.

ICD-10-CM to PheCode mapping — phecodes
text: data frame 90,768 rows 6 columns: ICD ICD-10 code Flag ICD version (always 10) ICDString ICD-10 description Phecode Phecode number PhecodeString Phecode description PhecodeCategory Disease category

title: ICD-10-CM to PheCode mapping — phecodes
text: Denny, J., Bastarache, L., Ritchie, M. et al. Systematic comparison phenome-wide association study electronic medical record data genome-wide association study data. Nat Biotechnol 31, 1102–1111 (2013). (doi:10.1038/nbt.2749 )

what: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_CCSR
title: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_CCSR
text: Determine association variable interest running Cox regression time event information logistic regression prevalence baseline. Generates PheWAS plots assess strenth associations

title: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_CCSR
code: phewas_analysis_CCSR(   cohort_df,   outcome_df = CCSR_outcomes,   target,   adj_vars = c("age", "sex"),   save_as = "CCSR",   n_event_cutoff = 10,   exclude_categories = c("Congenital malformations", "Injury and poisoning consequences",     "Health status factors", "Pregnancy and childbirth", "Perinatal conditions",     "Unacceptable principal diagnosis"),   one_to_one_mapping = TRUE,   exclude_history = TRUE,   return_tables = TRUE,   annotation_size = 5,   skip_colors = NULL )

title: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_CCSR
text: cohort_df dataframe including participant ID adjustment columns outcome_df Result CCSR_tte function target Analysis variable column name adj_vars List variables adjust models save_as Prefix saved plot names n_event_cutoff Diseases fewer events omitted exclude_categories CCSR categories omit exclude_history TRUE, considers events history baseline (default) skip_colors Omit colors palette, useful sync plots cohorts

title: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_CCSR
text: Returns two dataframes regression results generates PheWAS plots

title: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_phecodes
code: phewas_analysis_phecodes(   cohort_df = ghs_eps,   outcome_df = phecode_outcomes,   adj_vars = c("age", "sex"),   exclude_categories = c("congenital anomalies", "injuries & poisonings",     "pregnancy complications", "symptoms", "other"),   save_as = "phecode",   n_event_cutoff = 10,   target = target.scaled,   exclude_history = TRUE,   add_events = NULL,   add_events_name = NULL,   add_events_category = NULL,   return_tables = FALSE,   annotation_size = 5,   skip_colors = NULL,   plot_pdf = FALSE )

title: Determine the association of a variable of interest by running Cox regression on time to event information and logistic regression on prevalence at baseline. Generates PheWAS plots to assess the strenth of associations — phewas_analysis_phecodes
text: cohort_df dataframe including participant ID adjustment columns outcome_df Result phecode_tte function adj_vars List variables adjust models exclude_categories Phecode categories omit save_as Prefix saved plot names n_event_cutoff Diseases fewer events omitted target Analysis variable column name exclude_history TRUE, considers events history baseline (default) add_events Add user-generated event analysis. Needs exist outcome_df cohort_df dataframe skip_colors Omit colors palette, useful sync plots cohorts

title: Map temporal ICD-10 codes into outcomes for Cox regression using the CCSR convention — time_to_event_CCSR
text: dataframe_ID_BL dataframe participant ID column. Dataframe gets returned ICD-10 information added icd_table_long long table ICD-10 information, including ID, ICD-10 code date diagnosed tbl_mortality_contact dataframe containing participant ID, baseline visit date last contact/death date columns. censor_time Time events censored one_to_one_mapping false, map CCSR-ICD-10 pairs instead single default CCSR-ICD-10 pair save_table Decide dataframe written current directory
